---
title: "Setup and Quarto Practice"
format: html
editor: visual
embed-resources: true
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

## Libraries

To use an external *package* (a.k.a. a "library") we first need to install it.

In your **console** (NOT IN THIS QUARTO DOCUMENT!) run

> install.packages("tidyverse")

Once that is done, run the following code chunk:

```{r}
#| label: libraries

library(tidyverse)
```


To see if that worked, run the following without editing it:

```{r}
iris |>
  group_by(Species) |>
  summarize(
    mean_petal_length = mean(Petal.Length, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

Now, **render** this document.  Pay attention to where it is saved, and how the 
html file looks.
